framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Using high-throughput DFT (density functional theory) calculations to compute and publish the properties of thousands to millions of materials in a free, queryable database or open-source analysis library.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves someone can turn deep quantum-mechanical materials theory into infrastructure that thousands of other scientists and engineers rely on daily, rather than keeping the expertise locked in one lab's private results.

paths.what_it_is: Writing and long-term maintaining open-source molecular dynamics or simulation-analysis software (e.g., LAMMPS, OVITO) that becomes a standard tool across the materials modeling community.
paths.why_it_signals: Sustaining production-grade scientific software for a global user base demonstrates engineering discipline layered on top of materials science depth — a rarer combination than research output alone.
